speaker: KING
text: Though yet of Hamlet our dear brother’s death

speaker: KING
text: The memory be green, and that it us befitted

speaker: KING
text: To bear our hearts in grief, and our whole kingdom

speaker: KING
text: To be contracted in one brow of woe,

speaker: KING
text: Yet so far hath discretion fought with nature

speaker: KING
text: That we with wisest sorrow think on him

speaker: KING
text: Together with remembrance of ourselves.

speaker: KING
text: Therefore our sometime sister, now our queen,

speaker: KING
text: Th’ imperial jointress to this warlike state,

speaker: KING
text: Have we (as ’twere with a defeated joy,

speaker: KING
text: With an auspicious and a dropping eye,

speaker: KING
text: With mirth in funeral and with dirge in marriage,

speaker: KING
text: In equal scale weighing delight and dole)

speaker: KING
text: Taken to wife. Nor have we herein barred

speaker: KING
text: Your better wisdoms, which have freely gone

speaker: KING
text: With this affair along. For all, our thanks.

speaker: KING
text: Now follows that you know. Young Fortinbras,

speaker: KING
text: Holding a weak supposal of our worth

speaker: KING
text: Or thinking by our late dear brother’s death

speaker: KING
text: Our state to be disjoint and out of frame,

speaker: KING
text: Colleaguèd with this dream of his advantage,

speaker: KING
text: He hath not failed to pester us with message

speaker: KING
text: Importing the surrender of those lands

speaker: KING
text: Lost by his father, with all bonds of law,

speaker: KING
text: To our most valiant brother—so much for him.

speaker: KING
text: Now for ourself and for this time of meeting.

speaker: KING
text: Thus much the business is: we have here writ

speaker: KING
text: To Norway, uncle of young Fortinbras,

speaker: KING
text: Who, impotent and bedrid, scarcely hears

speaker: KING
text: Of this his nephew’s purpose, to suppress

speaker: KING
text: His further gait herein, in that the levies,

speaker: KING
text: The lists, and full proportions are all made

speaker: KING
text: Out of his subject; and we here dispatch

speaker: KING
text: You, good Cornelius, and you, Voltemand,

speaker: KING
text: For bearers of this greeting to old Norway,

speaker: KING
text: Giving to you no further personal power

speaker: KING
text: To business with the King more than the scope

speaker: KING
text: Of these dilated articles allow.

speaker: KING
text: Farewell, and let your haste commend your duty.

speaker:
CORNELIUS
              /
              VOLTEMAND
text: In that and all things will we show our duty.

speaker: KING
text: We doubt it nothing. Heartily farewell.

speaker: KING
text: And now, Laertes, what’s the news with you?

speaker: KING
text: You told us of some suit. What is ’t, Laertes?

speaker: KING
text: You cannot speak of reason to the Dane

speaker: KING
text: And lose your voice. What wouldst thou beg, Laertes,

speaker: KING
text: That shall not be my offer, not thy asking?

speaker: KING
text: The head is not more native to the heart,

speaker: KING
text: The hand more instrumental to the mouth,

speaker: KING
text: Than is the throne of Denmark to thy father.

speaker: KING
text: What wouldst thou have, Laertes?

speaker: LAERTES
text: My dread lord,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Your leave and favor to return to France,

speaker: LAERTES
text: From whence though willingly I came to Denmark

speaker: LAERTES
text: To show my duty in your coronation,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Yet now I must confess, that duty done,

speaker: LAERTES
text: My thoughts and wishes bend again toward France

speaker: LAERTES
text: And bow them to your gracious leave and pardon.

speaker: KING
text: Have you your father’s leave? What says Polonius?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Hath, my lord, wrung from me my slow leave

speaker: POLONIUS
text: By laborsome petition, and at last

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Upon his will I sealed my hard consent.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I do beseech you give him leave to go.

speaker: KING
text: Take thy fair hour, Laertes. Time be thine,

speaker: KING
text: And thy best graces spend it at thy will.—

speaker: KING
text: But now, my cousin Hamlet and my son—

speaker: HAMLET
text: A little more than kin and less than kind.

speaker: KING
text: How is it that the clouds still hang on you?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Not so, my lord; I am too much in the sun.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Good Hamlet, cast thy nighted color off,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And let thine eye look like a friend on Denmark.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Do not forever with thy vailèd lids

speaker: QUEEN
text: Seek for thy noble father in the dust.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Thou know’st ’tis common; all that lives must die,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Passing through nature to eternity.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, madam, it is common.

speaker: QUEEN
text: If it be,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Why seems it so particular with thee?

speaker: HAMLET
text: “Seems,” madam? Nay, it is. I know not “seems.”

speaker: HAMLET
text: ’Tis not alone my inky cloak, good mother,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor customary suits of solemn black,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor windy suspiration of forced breath,

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, nor the fruitful river in the eye,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor the dejected havior of the visage,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Together with all forms, moods, shapes of grief,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That can denote me truly. These indeed “seem,”

speaker: HAMLET
text: For they are actions that a man might play;

speaker: HAMLET
text: But I have that within which passes show,

speaker: HAMLET
text: These but the trappings and the suits of woe.

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis sweet and commendable in your nature, Hamlet,

speaker: KING
text: To give these mourning duties to your father.

speaker: KING
text: But you must know your father lost a father,

speaker: KING
text: That father lost, lost his, and the survivor bound

speaker: KING
text: In filial obligation for some term

speaker: KING
text: To do obsequious sorrow. But to persever

speaker: KING
text: In obstinate condolement is a course

speaker: KING
text: Of impious stubbornness. ’Tis unmanly grief.

speaker: KING
text: It shows a will most incorrect to heaven,

speaker: KING
text: A heart unfortified, a mind impatient,

speaker: KING
text: An understanding simple and unschooled.

speaker: KING
text: For what we know must be and is as common

speaker: KING
text: As any the most vulgar thing to sense,

speaker: KING
text: Why should we in our peevish opposition

speaker: KING
text: Take it to heart? Fie, ’tis a fault to heaven,

speaker: KING
text: A fault against the dead, a fault to nature,

speaker: KING
text: To reason most absurd, whose common theme

speaker: KING
text: Is death of fathers, and who still hath cried,

speaker: KING
text: From the first corse till he that died today,

speaker: KING
text: “This must be so.” We pray you, throw to earth

speaker: KING
text: This unprevailing woe and think of us

speaker: KING
text: As of a father; for let the world take note,

speaker: KING
text: You are the most immediate to our throne,

speaker: KING
text: And with no less nobility of love

speaker: KING
text: Than that which dearest father bears his son

speaker: KING
text: Do I impart toward you. For your intent

speaker: KING
text: In going back to school in Wittenberg,

speaker: KING
text: It is most retrograde to our desire,

speaker: KING
text: And we beseech you, bend you to remain

speaker: KING
text: Here in the cheer and comfort of our eye,

speaker: KING
text: Our chiefest courtier, cousin, and our son.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Let not thy mother lose her prayers, Hamlet.

speaker: QUEEN
text: I pray thee, stay with us. Go not to Wittenberg.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I shall in all my best obey you, madam.

speaker: KING
text: Why, ’tis a loving and a fair reply.

speaker: KING
text: Be as ourself in Denmark.—Madam, come.

speaker: KING
text: This gentle and unforced accord of Hamlet

speaker: KING
text: Sits smiling to my heart, in grace whereof

speaker: KING
text: No jocund health that Denmark drinks today

speaker: KING
text: But the great cannon to the clouds shall tell,

speaker: KING
text: And the King’s rouse the heaven shall bruit again,

speaker: KING
text: Respeaking earthly thunder. Come away.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, that this too, too sullied flesh would melt,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thaw, and resolve itself into a dew,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or that the Everlasting had not fixed

speaker: HAMLET
text: His canon ’gainst self-slaughter! O God, God,

speaker: HAMLET
text: How weary, stale, flat, and unprofitable

speaker: HAMLET
text: Seem to me all the uses of this world!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Fie on ’t, ah fie! ’Tis an unweeded garden

speaker: HAMLET
text: That grows to seed. Things rank and gross in nature

speaker: HAMLET
text: Possess it merely. That it should come to this:

speaker: HAMLET
text: But two months dead—nay, not so much, not two.

speaker: HAMLET
text: So excellent a king, that was to this

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hyperion to a satyr; so loving to my mother

speaker: HAMLET
text: That he might not beteem the winds of heaven

speaker: HAMLET
text: Visit her face too roughly. Heaven and Earth,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Must I remember? Why, she would hang on him

speaker: HAMLET
text: As if increase of appetite had grown

speaker: HAMLET
text: By what it fed on. And yet, within a month

speaker: HAMLET
text: (Let me not think on ’t; frailty, thy name is woman!),

speaker: HAMLET
text: A little month, or ere those shoes were old

speaker: HAMLET
text: With which she followed my poor father’s body,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Like Niobe, all tears—why she, even she

speaker: HAMLET
text: (O God, a beast that wants discourse of reason

speaker: HAMLET
text: Would have mourned longer!), married with my uncle,

speaker: HAMLET
text: My father’s brother, but no more like my father

speaker: HAMLET
text: Than I to Hercules. Within a month,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ere yet the salt of most unrighteous tears

speaker: HAMLET
text: Had left the flushing in her gallèd eyes,

speaker: HAMLET
text: She married. O, most wicked speed, to post

speaker: HAMLET
text: With such dexterity to incestuous sheets!

speaker: HAMLET
text: It is not, nor it cannot come to good.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But break, my heart, for I must hold my tongue.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Hail to your Lordship.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I am glad to see you well.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Horatio—or I do forget myself!

speaker: HORATIO
text: The same, my lord, and your poor servant ever.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sir, my good friend. I’ll change that name with you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: And what make you from Wittenberg, Horatio?—

speaker: HAMLET
text: Marcellus?

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: My good lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text:
I am very glad to see you.
                 
                To
                 
                Barnardo
                .
               Good even, sir.—

speaker: HAMLET
text: But what, in faith, make you from Wittenberg?

speaker: HORATIO
text: A truant disposition, good my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I would not hear your enemy say so,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor shall you do my ear that violence

speaker: HAMLET
text: To make it truster of your own report

speaker: HAMLET
text: Against yourself. I know you are no truant.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But what is your affair in Elsinore?

speaker: HAMLET
text: We’ll teach you to drink deep ere you depart.

speaker: HORATIO
text: My lord, I came to see your father’s funeral.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I prithee, do not mock me, fellow student.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I think it was to see my mother’s wedding.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Indeed, my lord, it followed hard upon.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thrift, thrift, Horatio. The funeral baked meats

speaker: HAMLET
text: Did coldly furnish forth the marriage tables.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Would I had met my dearest foe in heaven

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or ever I had seen that day, Horatio!

speaker: HAMLET
text: My father—methinks I see my father.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Where, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: In my mind’s eye, Horatio.

speaker: HORATIO
text: I saw him once. He was a goodly king.

speaker: HAMLET
text: He was a man. Take him for all in all,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I shall not look upon his like again.

speaker: HORATIO
text: My lord, I think I saw him yesternight.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Saw who?

speaker: HORATIO
text: My lord, the King your father.

speaker: HAMLET
text: The King my father?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Season your admiration for a while

speaker: HORATIO
text: With an attent ear, till I may deliver

speaker: HORATIO
text: Upon the witness of these gentlemen

speaker: HORATIO
text: This marvel to you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: For God’s love, let me hear!

speaker: HORATIO
text: Two nights together had these gentlemen,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Marcellus and Barnardo, on their watch,

speaker: HORATIO
text: In the dead waste and middle of the night,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Been thus encountered: a figure like your father,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Armed at point exactly, cap-à-pie,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Appears before them and with solemn march

speaker: HORATIO
text: Goes slow and stately by them. Thrice he walked

speaker: HORATIO
text: By their oppressed and fear-surprisèd eyes

speaker: HORATIO
text: Within his truncheon’s length, whilst they, distilled

speaker: HORATIO
text: Almost to jelly with the act of fear,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Stand dumb and speak not to him. This to me

speaker: HORATIO
text: In dreadful secrecy impart they did,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And I with them the third night kept the watch,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Where, as they had delivered, both in time,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Form of the thing (each word made true and good),

speaker: HORATIO
text: The apparition comes. I knew your father;

speaker: HORATIO
text: These hands are not more like.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But where was this?

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: My lord, upon the platform where we watch.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Did you not speak to it?

speaker: HORATIO
text: My lord, I did,

speaker: HORATIO
text: But answer made it none. Yet once methought

speaker: HORATIO
text: It lifted up its head and did address

speaker: HORATIO
text: Itself to motion, like as it would speak;

speaker: HORATIO
text: But even then the morning cock crew loud,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And at the sound it shrunk in haste away

speaker: HORATIO
text: And vanished from our sight.

speaker: HAMLET
text: ’Tis very strange.

speaker: HORATIO
text: As I do live, my honored lord, ’tis true.

speaker: HORATIO
text: And we did think it writ down in our duty

speaker: HORATIO
text: To let you know of it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Indeed, sirs, but this troubles me.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hold you the watch tonight?

speaker: ALL
text: We do, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Armed, say you?

speaker: ALL
text: Armed, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: From top to toe?

speaker: ALL
text: My lord, from head to foot.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then saw you not his face?

speaker: HORATIO
text: O, yes, my lord, he wore his beaver up.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What, looked he frowningly?

speaker: HORATIO
text: A countenance more in sorrow than in anger.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Pale or red?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Nay, very pale.

speaker: HAMLET
text: And fixed his eyes upon you?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Most constantly.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I would I had been there.

speaker: HORATIO
text: It would have much amazed you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Very like. Stayed it long?

speaker: HORATIO
text: While one with moderate haste might tell a hundred.

speaker:
BARNARDO
              /
              MARCELLUS
text: Longer, longer.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Not when I saw ’t.

speaker: HAMLET
text: His beard was grizzled, no?

speaker: HORATIO
text: It was as I have seen it in his life,

speaker: HORATIO
text: A sable silvered.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will watch tonight.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Perchance ’twill walk again.

speaker: HORATIO
text: I warrant it will.

speaker: HAMLET
text: If it assume my noble father’s person,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll speak to it, though hell itself should gape

speaker: HAMLET
text: And bid me hold my peace. I pray you all,

speaker: HAMLET
text: If you have hitherto concealed this sight,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let it be tenable in your silence still;

speaker: HAMLET
text: And whatsomever else shall hap tonight,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Give it an understanding but no tongue.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will requite your loves. So fare you well.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Upon the platform, ’twixt eleven and twelve,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll visit you.

speaker: ALL
text: Our duty to your Honor.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Your loves, as mine to you. Farewell.

speaker: HAMLET
text: My father’s spirit—in arms! All is not well.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I doubt some foul play. Would the night were come!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Till then, sit still, my soul. Foul deeds will rise,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Though all the earth o’erwhelm them, to men’s eyes.
